author: Xxxii
text:
Delivery plugin §16 doctor hook: code-worktree drift audit

bl-f813 shipped bl-delivery without its §16 doctor hook, so the CODE-worktree
drift class was implemented nowhere: base doctor (bl-d024) deliberately cannot
see it (it reads only the §8 change scratch; the code worktree lives in the
delivery plugin's own XDG territory at a path base never derives), and the
plugin that owns that territory had no doctor arm.

- Add 'doctor' to bl-delivery's PROTOCOL_JSON ops so 'bl install' wires + validates the hook.
- New delivery_doctor module: audit() partitions the actor's still-claimed balls
  against the materialized worktrees in this binding's territory. A claimed id with
  no worktree => missing (fix: bl prime); a worktree with no live claim => orphan
  (fix: the named git worktree remove, human-run). render() prints to the doctor
  stream (no return channel, §7); does NOT reuse base doctor's Report (its header
  speaks of 'core-owned' findings).
- Claimed set reuses the actor-scoped delivery_repo::claimed_ids (the same set
  prime re-materializes), so only this actor's claims can drift here.
- bl-delivery doctor() runs the audit once on pre (post is a no-op); branches off
  before the project repo is opened, since the audit never opens it.
- 100% coverage, all files <300 lines.
